title: Review: feat all backends + CI + release docs
notes:
Maintainer risk: HIGH — Five P0 memory-safety/zeroization bugs (RSA key clone, unzeroized token, serde_json intermediates, pkcs11 silent fallback, KMS panic path) that would generate CVEs or hard-to-debug failures in production. Multiple P1 API design issues that will be hard to change post-0.1.0 (async from_uri, NotFound from put(), name param semantics). The cloud-credential-chain issue (azure-kv) is a silent privilege escalation in prod environments.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) did not run the code or read every line of the AWS/Azure/GCP SDK internals referenced; (2) the pkcs11 and wolfhsm backends have incomplete implementations that will have more bugs when filled in; (3) the bitwarden auth caching path was not traced through the BWS SDK fully. Would raise confidence if: cargo test --all-features passes clean; Miri clean run over secretx-core and secretx-local-signing; read AWS SDK Blob zeroization guarantees.
